data.description:
Graduate src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py (867 LOC, over the 800 soft cap) off the module-size allowlist by extracting the cryptographic-identity & signed-attestation fold cluster into a NEW pure, stdlib-free leaf src/rebar/reducer/_processors_identity.py.

MOVE (by name): _fold_author_attribution, _rederive_keyring_keys, process_key_event, _bootstrap_genesis_keyring, attestation_kind, process_signature. These reference only each other (a true leaf): process_key_event calls _rederive_keyring_keys; process_signature calls attestation_kind and _fold_author_attribution. _processors.py imports them back and re-exports them (# noqa: F401) so rebar.reducer._processors.<symbol> keeps resolving for the reducer dispatch table (_replay.py imports process_key_event + process_signature from ._processors) and for attestation_kind's importers (signing.py, _commands/verify_opcert.py, _opcert_signing.py, and tests). reducer/__init__.py needs no change.

DEVIATION from the docs/architecture.md offenders row: that row names the seam _processors_keys.py with ONLY the 4-function keys cluster and explicitly flags it SUB-FLOOR (~84 LOC, below the 100-LOC leaf floor). This ticket WIDENS the seam to also move the attestation pair (attestation_kind + process_signature) so the new leaf clears the 100-LOC floor, and names it _processors_identity.py. _fold_author_attribution MUST travel with process_signature (its caller) into the leaf, else a back-edge cycle forms. The sub-floor _processors_keys.py row is stale/superseded and is DELETED by this ticket.

This is a pure/mechanical refactor: no signature or logic changes; E5 testing-plan is not-applicable — the existing reducer/attestation/signing test suites are the oracle.

Acceptance criteria (observable, in-session-verifiable):
- New leaf src/rebar/reducer/_processors_identity.py is 100-800 LOC (wc -l).
- src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py is <=800 LOC (wc -l), down from 867.
- The 6 named symbols live in the leaf and are re-exported (# noqa: F401) from _processors.py so rebar.reducer._processors still exposes them unchanged (back-compat via re-export).
- _processors.py removed from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt; its docs/architecture.md offenders-table row deleted.
- make lint passes (ruff check + ruff format --check).
- tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py passes (allowlist == over-cap set == ceilings keys).
- python -m pytest tests/unit/test_attestations_reducer.py tests/unit/test_signing.py tests/unit/test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py -q passes, and python -m pytest tests/unit -k 'reducer or attestation or signing' -q passes.
data.title: Split reducer/_processors.py — extract identity & attestation fold cluster into _processors_identity.py
